title: When Earth Was an Asteroid Rain Hell
gist: Curtin University researchers modeled the heat dynamics of early Earth during the Hadean period (~4 billion years ago), publishing in Science, and found that intense asteroid bombardment delivered so much heat to the mantle that solid crust struggled to form.

themes.theme: Asteroid bombardment and the delayed formation of Earth's crust

insights.claim: As the outer gas giants settled into their orbits roughly 4.5 billion years ago, the shifting gravitational forces redirected leftover rocky debris from the solar system's formation toward the inner planets, subjecting both Earth and the Moon to an unrelenting cosmic bombardment.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: Frames the mechanism (gas-giant orbital settling) that triggered the early bombardment, providing the causal chain behind the planet's turbulent formative period.
insights.quote: As the outer gas giants settled into their orbits, the shifting gravitational forces sent this debris screaming toward the inner planets.
insights.locator: After the planets coalesced around 4.5 billion years ago

insights.claim: Curtin University researchers in Australia modeled the heat dynamics of early Earth during the Hadean period (roughly 4 billion years ago), incorporating the energy delivered by asteroid strikes, and published their analysis in the journal Science.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: Identifies the specific study and methodology behind the finding, giving a citable source for anyone tracking early-Earth geodynamic modeling research.
insights.locator: Researchers from Curtin University in Australia

insights.claim: The intense period of asteroid impacts during the Hadean delivered so much heat to early Earth's mantle that solid crust had a difficult time forming.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: The study's central causal claim — establishes asteroid-driven heat flux as a rate-limiting factor for early continental crust formation, relevant to models of when Earth became geologically and biologically habitable.
insights.quote: the intense period of impacts delivered so much heat to the mantle of our young planet that solid crust had a difficult time forming
insights.locator: They determined the intense period of impacts
